subject: Re: Range of CTF and Motion for tilt-series pre-processing
body:
Hi Yuqi,

This is data from a tilt series, right, hence the exposure per frame is quite low and pixel size quite large.

Is your problem that the defocus and estimated resolution values in the Overview are much higher than you expected?
For tilt series, if the data is good and the tilt series was tracked well during collection, you would expect the defocus to be stable for each tilt series, and the estimated resolution and average motion should slightly increase as the tilt angle increases.
Yours doesn’t look like that, but I don’t know what kind of tilt scheme or anything about this data.


For CTF, I would suggest:
Click on the Fourier Space tab at the top.
Look at the fit of CTF to the power spectrum of individual micrographs.
Does the fit look correct to you?
If the fit looks good, then your data is just high defocus and low dose, a tilt frame has only a fraction of the signal as a single tilt movie.
If the fit looks wrong, let’s correct it:
The 1D power spectrum shows you the Range in grey. If you put your mouse cursor over the plot it will tell you the wavelength in Å.
Adjust your range so that it starts just before the first drop to zero, and finishes where the Thon rings disappear.
Click Process Only This Item’s CTF at the top, and you can keep changing your settings
Increase Window size further to get some more signal in to your power spectrum.
Try adjusting Range and Defocus ranges until the fit seems correct - it shouldn’t take long, just make the range suitable so the fit doesn’t do something crazy. 
You can check your new settings are good for a few micrographs, then process all of them when you are happy.

For motion, the online tutorial http://www.warpem.com/warp/?page_id=185 <http://www.warpem.com/warp/?page_id=185> says:
The default range of 0.05–0.25 [Ny] is usually ok for data with 1.00–1.35 Å/px. For larger pixels, consider increasing the range.
The more negative the B-factor, the less the higher spatial frequencies will be considered.
So because you have slightly large pixel size and not so much high frequency info, you could try slightly increasing the range to low frequencies and more negative B-factor.

Your exposure rate on the camera pixel is 0.36e/Å2/frame * 4.41Å2/px = 1.59e/px/frame (am I right?) so your motion setting of 1x1x4 seems fine if there are 4 frames in each movie. I don’t have enough experience to say whether combining frames (i.e. 1x1x2) would help or hinder.
Finally, you can use the Real Space tab and click on the box to show motion traces. I think it is hard to tell if the motion correction is working well, but you can have a look.

All the best
Teige


> On 24 Mar 2021, at 12:45, yuqi zhang <824586412@qq.com> wrote:
> 
> Hi, all. I have a simple question. When I do the CTF and motion for tilt-series pre-processing. I didn't know how to specify the range of CTF and motion (in Ny or A). The result values of estimated resolution and defocus, in my opinion, may be a little bad. The detector was K3 and the pixel size was 2.1A. 
> 
> The settings was in the picture. And I wanted to know whether I should change the parameter(CTF range / Motion Consider / weight with B / Models, spacially these four, I thought.)
> 
> --
